steps.title: Understand the function notation
steps.content:
The function $s(t)$ gives the approximate mass of this isotope, in grams, that remains $t$ years after the sample starts to decay.
So $s(t)$ outputs the remaining mass (grams), with $t$ representing time (years).

steps.title: Apply the equation and match the answer
steps.content:
$s(93) = 31$ tells us: when $t = 93$ years, the remaining mass is 31 grams.
This is consistent with the decay model: 93 years is 3 half-lives, so the remaining mass is $248 \times (0.5)^3 = 31$ grams ✓

This interpretation exactly matches Choice A — Approximately $31$ grams of the sample remains $93$ years after the sample starts to decay.: Approximately 31 grams of the sample remains 93 years after the sample starts to decay. That matches Choice A — Approximately $31$ grams of the sample remains $93$ years after the sample starts to decay..
